posts.text:
A huge surplus in global oil markets going into the Hormuz crisis provided a major cushion against the initial shock

But with global oil inventories falling at a record pace since then, the recovery in flows through the Strait has come at a critical time: https://t.co/vvjkiIlFD9 https://t.co/xKRDhCpMjb
posts.summary: Global oil inventories have fallen at a record pace since the Hormuz crisis, depleting the surplus cushion that initially absorbed the shock, making the recent recovery of flows through the Strait critically important for market stability.

posts.text:
The supply response to the Hormuz crisis extends well beyond the Middle East

Led by the US, Atlantic Basin producers have boosted exports to Asia. At the same time, China has drawn on inventories to curb crude imports, helping ease wider market pressures: https://t.co/vvjkiIlFD9 https://t.co/nEd15sIyjT
posts.summary: The oil market is adjusting to Middle East supply concerns through increased US and Atlantic Basin exports to Asia, while China's drawdown of strategic reserves is reducing global crude demand pressures. These supply and demand responses are helping to stabilize broader market conditions amid the Hormuz strait crisis.

posts.text: Canada and Japan are deepening cooperation on critical minerals as both countries seek to reduce reliance on China’s supply chain dominance. The partnership could include joint stockpiling, new mining projects, and expanded energy exports. #CriticalMinerals #Canada #Japan

posts.text: U.S. GAS production, consumption and exports appear to have been balanced for the last two months, stabilising inventories after a steady accumulation earlier in the year. Inventories were 173 billion cubic feet (+7% or +0.47 standard deviations) above the ten-year seasonal https://t.co/TXoLf22aj5
posts.summary: U.S. natural gas supply and demand have reached equilibrium over the past two months, halting the earlier inventory buildup, with current stockpiles running 7% above the ten-year seasonal average. This stabilization suggests the market has found balance after a period of excess accumulation.

posts.text: Big Oil’s green pivot is losing momentum. Faced with investor pressure, weaker renewable returns, and stronger oil & gas profits, major energy companies are shifting capital back to hydrocarbons while taking a more selective approach to low-carbon investments. #Oil
posts.summary: Major oil and gas companies are redirecting capital away from renewable energy projects and back toward hydrocarbon investments due to investor pressure and more attractive returns in traditional energy sectors. This shift suggests a pullback in the industry's energy transition commitments in favor of near-term fossil fuel profitability.

posts.text:

posts.text: After a one day respite, the June Swoon for crude and gasoline prices has resumed. Retail gasoline prices barely top $3.90/gal and look to target $3.75-$3.80/gal averages for July 4th weekend. Diesel plunge likely to accelerate as well.
posts.summary: Crude and gasoline prices are declining as the "June Swoon" resumes, with retail gasoline expected to average $3.75-$3.80 per gallon by the July 4th weekend and diesel prices anticipated to fall further. The oil market is experiencing downward price pressure across multiple fuel types.

posts.text: Russia may be about to import gasoline cargoes from India, thanks to its damaged refining infrastructure. They are probably short about 125,000 b/d of motor fuel and India typically exports about four times that amount. Ukrainian attacks on Russia are creating strange alliances.
posts.summary: Russia's damaged refining capacity has created a shortage of approximately 125,000 barrels per day of gasoline, potentially driving imports from India, which has sufficient export capacity to meet this demand. This supply disruption reflects the impact of Ukrainian attacks on Russian energy infrastructure and is reshaping global oil trade dynamics.

posts.text:
China has not stopped buying crude. It has stopped buying crude for now.

That distinction is worth billions in positioning if you get it right.

Our latest analysis tracks what is actually happening inside Chinese crude demand, and why the oversupply narrative building in the https://t.co/ayJaLDSXl6

The distinction between a pause and a cessation could have substantial financial implications as the market debates broader oversupply concerns.
